diagrams.title: Fundamental Relational Algebra Operations
diagrams.description: A visual diagram showing the six fundamental operations of relational algebra arranged in two rows: the traditional set operations (Union, Intersection, Difference, Cartesian Product) and the special relational operations (Selection, Projection, Join, Division). Each operation is shown as a box with input tables entering from the left and output tables exiting to the right, demonstrating how data flows through each operation.
diagrams.purpose: Introduces the core operations of relational algebra showing how they transform one or two input relations into an output relation, establishing the foundational vocabulary for the entire topic

diagrams.title: Join Operations Visualization
diagrams.description: A diagram showing different types of joins with two example tables - Employee(EmpID, Name, DeptID) and Department(DeptID, DeptName). The diagram shows: (1) Natural Join result matching on DeptID, (2) Theta Join with a condition (Salary > 50000), (3) Left Outer Join showing all employees with NULL for missing departments, (4) Right Outer Join showing all departments with NULL for employees without matching DeptID. Each join type is illustrated with shaded regions in Venn diagrams showing which tuples are included.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the different join operations which are among the most important and frequently used operations in relational algebra, helping students understand how tuples are combined from related tables

diagrams.title: Relational Algebra Expression Tree
diagrams.description: A tree diagram showing a step-by-step decomposition of a complex query: 'Find names of employees who work in the Marketing department'. The tree is structured bottom-up with leaf nodes showing the base relations (Employee, Department, WorksOn), intermediate nodes showing operations (Selection, Projection, Join), and the root showing the final result. Arrows connect parent-child nodes showing the data flow from leaves to root.
elements: Final result table showing employee names
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how complex relational algebra queries are constructed and evaluated using expression trees, showing the procedural nature of relational algebra and how multiple operations combine to answer a query
